1→/**
     2→ * @file saga_coordinator.h
     3→ *
     4→ * @brief Core abstraction for orchestrating distributed transactions
     5→ *        via the Saga Pattern inside EduPay Ledger Academy.
     6→ *
     7→ *  The coordinator lives in the domain layer (Enterprise-Business Rules)
     8→ *  and therefore must not import any framework-specific headers.
     9→ *  It exposes a small surface-area in C so that professors can swap
    10→ *  infrastructure (Kafka, NATS, gRPC, REST, SQL, NoSQL, etc.) without
    11→ *  recompiling core business rules.
    12→ *
    13→ *  Thread-safety:
    14→ *      All public functions are re-entrant but *not* thread-safe.
    15→ *      Callers that share a coordinator instance *must* provide their own
    16→ *      synchronization (e.g., inject a mutex from the application layer).
    17→ *
    18→ *  Memory-management:
    19→ *      Memory is allocated only inside saga_coordinator_create().
    20→ *      The caller is responsible for releasing resources via
    21→ *      saga_coordinator_destroy().
    22→ *
    23→ *  License:
    24→ *      MIT – Copyright © 2024 EduPay Ledger Academy
    25→ */
    26→
    27→#ifndef EDU_PAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_COORDINATOR_H
    28→#define EDU_PAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_COORDINATOR_H
    29→
    30→/*------------------------------ System Headers -----------------------------*/
    31→#include <stdint.h>
    32→#include <stdbool.h>
    33→#include <stddef.h>
    34→
    35→/*------------------------------ Public Macros ------------------------------*/
    36→#define SAGA_MAX_STEPS        32     /* Upper bound to keep stack usage low */
    37→#define SAGA_MAX_ERROR_TEXT  128     /* Human-readable reason buffer size   */
    38→
    39→/*------------------------------- Enumerations ------------------------------*/
    40→/**
    41→ * @enum saga_step_state_t
    42→ * Represents runtime state of an individual step during a Saga execution.
    43→ */
    44→typedef enum
    45→{
    46→    SAGA_STEP_PENDING = 0,     /* Step has not been executed yet              */
    47→    SAGA_STEP_COMPLETED,       /* Step executed successfully                  */
    48→    SAGA_STEP_FAILED,          /* Step executed and returned error            */
    49→    SAGA_STEP_COMPENSATED,     /* Compensation executed after a failure       */
    50→    SAGA_STEP_COMPENSATION_FAILED /* Compensation itself failed              */
    51→} saga_step_state_t;
    52→
    53→/**
    54→ * @enum saga_execution_state_t
    55→ * High-level status of the overall Saga.
    56→ */
    57→typedef enum
    58→{
    59→    SAGA_STATE_IDLE = 0,
    60→    SAGA_STATE_RUNNING,
    61→    SAGA_STATE_ROLLING_BACK,
    62→    SAGA_STATE_COMPLETED,
    63→    SAGA_STATE_ABORTED
    64→} saga_execution_state_t;
    65→
    66→/*------------------------------ Forward Decls ------------------------------*/
    67→struct saga_coordinator_t;
    68→
    69→/**
    70→ * Callback invoked by a Saga step to perform its forward (happy-path) logic.
    71→ *
    72→ * @param step_ctx  User-supplied context pointer bound to the step.
    73→ * @param reason    Output buffer for an error description if the step fails.
    74→ * @param reason_len Length of the provided buffer.
    75→ *
    76→ * @return true  if the step succeeded and the Saga may continue.
    77→ *         false if an error occurred; compensation will be triggered.
    78→ */
    79→typedef bool (*saga_forward_cb_t)(void *step_ctx,
    80→                                  char *reason,
    81→                                  size_t reason_len);
    82→
    83→/**
    84→ * Callback invoked during rollback when a previous forward step failed.
    85→ *
    86→ * @param step_ctx  Same context pointer passed to the forward call.
    87→ * @param reason    Output buffer for an error description if compensation
    88→ *                  fails. When compensation fails, the Saga transitions to
    89→ *                  SAGA_STATE_ABORTED and no further steps are attempted.
    90→ * @param reason_len Length of the provided buffer.
    91→ *
    92→ * @return true  if compensation succeeded.
    93→ *         false if compensation itself failed – requires manual intervention.
    94→ */
    95→typedef bool (*saga_compensate_cb_t)(void *step_ctx,
    96→                                     char *reason,
    97→                                     size_t reason_len);
    98→
    99→/*------------------------------ Data Structures ----------------------------*/
   100→/**
   101→ * @struct saga_step_t
   102→ * Encapsulates a single unit of work and its compensating action.
   103→ */
   104→typedef struct
   105→{
   106→    const char          *name;           /* Human-readable step identifier   */
   107→    saga_forward_cb_t    do_txn;         /* Forward transaction logic        */
   108→    saga_compensate_cb_t undo_txn;       /* Compensation logic               */
   109→    void                *ctx;            /* User-defined context per step    */
   110→    saga_step_state_t    state;          /* Runtime state                    */
   111→    char                 error[SAGA_MAX_ERROR_TEXT]; /* Error buffer         */
   112→} saga_step_t;
   113→
   114→/**
   115→ * @struct saga_coordinator_t
   116→ * Public handle used by application services to compose a Saga.
   117→ */
   118→typedef struct saga_coordinator_t
   119→{
   120→    saga_step_t            steps[SAGA_MAX_STEPS];
   121→    uint8_t                step_count;
   122→    uint8_t                current;      /* Index of currently executing step */
   123→    saga_execution_state_t state;
   124→} saga_coordinator_t;
   125→
   126→/*--------------------------- Public API Functions --------------------------*/
   127→
   128→/**
   129→ * Create a fresh Saga coordinator.
   130→ *
   131→ * @param[out] out_handle Pointer to hold the newly-allocated coordinator.
   132→ *
   133→ * @return true on success; false if memory allocation fails.
   134→ */
   135→bool saga_coordinator_create(saga_coordinator_t **out_handle);
   136→
   137→/**
   138→ * Append a step to the Saga.
   139→ *
   140→ * @param saga     Saga instance obtained from saga_coordinator_create().
   141→ * @param name     Null-terminated string used for logging/observability.
   142→ * @param forward  Forward (commit) callback.
   143→ * @param compensate Compensation (rollback) callback.
   144→ * @param ctx      User context pointer passed untouched to both callbacks.
   145→ *
   146→ * @return true if the step was registered; false if capacity reached.
   147→ */
   148→bool saga_coordinator_add_step(saga_coordinator_t *saga,
   149→                               const char        *name,
   150→                               saga_forward_cb_t  forward,
   151→                               saga_compensate_cb_t compensate,
   152→                               void              *ctx);
   153→
   154→/**
   155→ * Execute the Saga end-to-end.
   156→ *
   157→ * The function iterates over all steps:
   158→ *   1. Invokes each forward callback sequentially.
   159→ *   2. On first failure, switches to rollback mode and calls compensation
   160→ *      callbacks in reverse order for *completed* steps.
   161→ *
   162→ * @param saga  Saga handle.
   163→ *
   164→ * @return SAGA_STATE_COMPLETED on success,
   165→ *         SAGA_STATE_ABORTED   if any compensation fails,
   166→ *         SAGA_STATE_ROLLING_BACK if rollback succeeded but original
   167→ *                                 forward path had a failure.
   168→ */
   169→saga_execution_state_t saga_coordinator_execute(saga_coordinator_t *saga);
   170→
   171→/**
   172→ * Acquire details about the failure that caused a rollback or abort.
   173→ *
   174→ * @param saga  Saga handle.
   175→ *
   176→ * @return Pointer to a constant string describing the last error,
   177→ *         or NULL if no error was recorded.
   178→ *
   179→ * @note The pointer becomes invalid after saga_coordinator_destroy().
   180→ */
   181→const char * saga_coordinator_last_error(const saga_coordinator_t *saga);
   182→
   183→/**
   184→ * Release all resources held by the coordinator.
   185→ *
   186→ * @param saga  Pointer returned by saga_coordinator_create().
   187→ */
   188→void saga_coordinator_destroy(saga_coordinator_t *saga);
   189→
   190→/*----------------------- Inline Convenience Functions ----------------------*/
   191→static inline bool saga_is_success(const saga_execution_state_t st)
   192→{
   193→    return st == SAGA_STATE_COMPLETED;
   194→}
   195→
   196→static inline bool saga_is_failed(const saga_execution_state_t st)
   197→{
   198→    return (st == SAGA_STATE_ROLLING_BACK) || (st == SAGA_STATE_ABORTED);
   199→}
   200→
   201→#endif /* EDU_PAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_COORDINATOR_H */
   202→

     2→/**
     3→ * ============================================================================
     4→ *  EduPay Ledger Academy – Core Domain (Saga Pattern Definitions)
     5→ *  File: saga_definition.h
     6→ *
     7→ *  Description:
     8→ *    Public, framework-agnostic definitions for the Saga Pattern used within
     9→ *    EduPay Ledger Academy.  These interfaces live in the core domain layer so
    10→ *    that professors can swap out messaging middleware (e.g., NATS, Kafka,
    11→ *    AMQP) or persistence engines (e.g., PostgreSQL, DynamoDB) without touching
    12→ *    business rules.
    13→ *
    14→ *  The definitions below purposely avoid leaking infrastructure concerns
    15→ *  (threads, sockets, ORMs, etc.).  They only describe *what* a saga is and
    16→ *  *how* steps are composed.  Concrete executors, adapters, and integration
    17→ *  tests are implemented in the application / infrastructure layers.
    18→ *
    19→ *  Author: EduPay Ledger Academy Core Team
    20→ *  SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
    21→ * ============================================================================
    22→ */
    23→
    24→#ifndef EDUPAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_DEFINITION_H
    25→#define EDUPAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_DEFINITION_H
    26→
    27→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28→ *  Standard Library
    29→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    30→#include <stdint.h>
    31→#include <stdbool.h>
    32→#include <stddef.h>
    33→
    34→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35→ *  Versioning
    36→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    37→#define SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_MAJOR 1
    38→#define SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_MINOR 0
    39→#define SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_PATCH 0
    40→
    41→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42→ *  Constants & Configuration
    43→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    44→
    45→/* Upper bound prevents memory corruption in student exercises.   */
    46→#ifndef SAGA_MAX_STEPS
    47→#define SAGA_MAX_STEPS 16U
    48→#endif /* SAGA_MAX_STEPS */
    49→
    50→/* Status/Return codes for saga operations.                      */
    51→typedef enum
    52→{
    53→    SAGA_OK = 0,
    54→    SAGA_ERR_INVALID_ARG      = -1,
    55→    SAGA_ERR_TOO_MANY_STEPS   = -2,
    56→    SAGA_ERR_ALREADY_STARTED  = -3,
    57→    SAGA_ERR_STEP_FAILED      = -4,
    58→    SAGA_ERR_COMPENSATION     = -5,
    59→    SAGA_ERR_NOT_STARTED      = -6
    60→} saga_rc_t;
    61→
    62→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63→ *  Forward Declarations
    64→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    65→struct saga_step;
    66→struct saga_definition;
    67→
    68→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69→ *  Function Pointer Types
    70→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    71→/**
    72→ * Each saga step is implemented as a pair of callback functions that operate
    73→ * on an opaque, caller-supplied context.  Business rules live inside these
    74→ * callbacks, whereas the saga engine merely orchestrates them.
    75→ *
    76→ * Return:  SAGA_OK (0)     – success
    77→ *          < 0             – error code, triggers compensation
    78→ */
    79→typedef saga_rc_t (*saga_step_action_fn)(void *opaque_ctx);
    80→
    81→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    82→ *  Enumerations
    83→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    84→typedef enum
    85→{
    86→    SAGA_STEP_PENDING = 0,
    87→    SAGA_STEP_COMPLETED,
    88→    SAGA_STEP_FAILED,
    89→    SAGA_STEP_COMPENSATED
    90→} saga_step_state_t;
    91→
    92→typedef enum
    93→{
    94→    SAGA_PENDING = 0,
    95→    SAGA_IN_PROGRESS,
    96→    SAGA_COMPLETED,
    97→    SAGA_ROLLING_BACK,
    98→    SAGA_COMPENSATED,
    99→    SAGA_ABORTED
   100→} saga_state_t;
   101→
   102→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103→ *  Data Structures
   104→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   105→
   106→/**
   107→ * saga_step:
   108→ *    Declarative description of a single unit of work within a saga.
   109→ *
   110→ * Members:
   111→ *    id                 – unique identifier within the saga (array index).
   112→ *    name               – human-readable identifier (for audit logs).
   113→ *    forward_action     – executes the primary business logic.
   114→ *    compensation_action– reverses forward_action if failure occurs.
   115→ *    state              – runtime state tracked by saga executor.
   116→ */
   117→typedef struct saga_step
   118→{
   119→    uint8_t               id;
   120→    const char           *name;
   121→    saga_step_action_fn   forward_action;
   122→    saga_step_action_fn   compensation_action;
   123→    saga_step_state_t     state;
   124→} saga_step_t;
   125→
   126→/**
   127→ * saga_definition:
   128→ *    Immutable blueprint for constructing a saga at runtime.  A concrete
   129→ *    executor copies this definition into a mutable saga_instance that tracks
   130→ *    states, timing, and metrics.
   131→ *
   132→ * Members:
   133→ *    saga_name          – business-level identifier (e.g., "TuitionPayment").
   134→ *    version            – semantic versioning so code & DB migrations align.
   135→ *    steps              – fixed-size array of steps.
   136→ *    step_count         – number of valid steps stored in ::steps.
   137→ *
   138→ *    NOTE:  The struct is intentionally POD-style so that it can be serialized
   139→ *           into JSON/YAML/TOML for interactive labs or persisted in the
   140→ *           Audit Trail event stream without custom marshallers.
   141→ */
   142→typedef struct saga_definition
   143→{
   144→    const char    *saga_name;
   145→    struct
   146→    {
   147→        uint16_t major;
   148→        uint16_t minor;
   149→        uint16_t patch;
   150→    } version;
   151→    saga_step_t   steps[SAGA_MAX_STEPS];
   152→    uint8_t       step_count;
   153→} saga_definition_t;
   154→
   155→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   156→ *  API – Compile-Time Construction Helpers
   157→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   158→
   159→/**
   160→ * SAGA_DEF_INIT:
   161→ *    Macro to create an empty saga_definition_t literal at compile time.
   162→ */
   163→#define SAGA_DEF_INIT(NAME)                      \
   164→    {                                            \
   165→        .saga_name  = NAME,                      \
   166→        .version    = {                          \
   167→            .major = SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_MAJOR, \
   168→            .minor = SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_MINOR, \
   169→            .patch = SAGA_DEFINITION_VERSION_PATCH  \
   170→        },                                       \
   171→        .steps      = {0},                       \
   172→        .step_count = 0                          \
   173→    }
   174→
   175→/**
   176→ * saga_def_add_step:
   177→ *    Adds a step to a saga_definition.  Safe for compile-time or run-time use.
   178→ *
   179→ * Parameters:
   180→ *    def                 – pointer to saga_definition_t.
   181→ *    step_name           – constant string, must outlive saga_definition_t.
   182→ *    forward_fn          – required forward action.
   183→ *    compensation_fn     – optional compensation (can be NULL).
   184→ *
   185→ * Returns:
   186→ *    SAGA_OK on success, otherwise negative error code.
   187→ */
   188→static inline saga_rc_t
   189→saga_def_add_step(saga_definition_t     *def,
   190→                  const char            *step_name,
   191→                  saga_step_action_fn    forward_fn,
   192→                  saga_step_action_fn    compensation_fn)
   193→{
   194→    if (def == NULL || step_name == NULL || forward_fn == NULL)
   195→        return SAGA_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
   196→
   197→    if (def->step_count >= SAGA_MAX_STEPS)
   198→        return SAGA_ERR_TOO_MANY_STEPS;
   199→
   200→    uint8_t idx = def->step_count;
   201→
   202→    def->steps[idx].id                  = idx;
   203→    def->steps[idx].name                = step_name;
   204→    def->steps[idx].forward_action      = forward_fn;
   205→    def->steps[idx].compensation_action = compensation_fn;
   206→    def->steps[idx].state               = SAGA_STEP_PENDING;
   207→
   208→    def->step_count++;
   209→    return SAGA_OK;
   210→}
   211→
   212→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   213→ *  API – Introspection Utilities
   214→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   215→
   216→/**
   217→ * saga_def_get_step:
   218→ *    Retrieves a mutable pointer to a step by ID.
   219→ */
   220→static inline saga_step_t *
   221→saga_def_get_step(saga_definition_t *def, uint8_t id)
   222→{
   223→    if (def == NULL || id >= def->step_count)
   224→        return NULL;
   225→    return &def->steps[id];
   226→}
   227→
   228→/**
   229→ * saga_def_find_step_by_name:
   230→ *    Linear search is adequate because compile-time bound is small
   231→ *    (≤ SAGA_MAX_STEPS).  Students can refactor to hashmap for bonus credit.
   232→ */
   233→static inline saga_step_t *
   234→saga_def_find_step_by_name(saga_definition_t *def, const char *name)
   235→{
   236→    if (def == NULL || name == NULL)
   237→        return NULL;
   238→
   239→    for (uint8_t i = 0; i < def->step_count; i++)
   240→    {
   241→        if (def->steps[i].name && (strcmp(def->steps[i].name, name) == 0))
   242→            return &def->steps[i];
   243→    }
   244→    return NULL;
   245→}
   246→
   247→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   248→ *  API – Validation
   249→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   250→
   251→/**
   252→ * saga_def_validate:
   253→ *    Performs a lightweight, synchronous verification that the saga
   254→ *    definition is internally consistent before handing it off to an
   255→ *    asynchronous executor.
   256→ *
   257→ * Returns:
   258→ *    SAGA_OK              – definition passes sanity checks
   259→ *    SAGA_ERR_INVALID_ARG – null pointer or missing callbacks
   260→ */
   261→static inline saga_rc_t
   262→saga_def_validate(const saga_definition_t *def)
   263→{
   264→    if (def == NULL || def->step_count == 0)
   265→        return SAGA_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
   266→
   267→    for (uint8_t i = 0; i < def->step_count; i++)
   268→    {
   269→        const saga_step_t *s = &def->steps[i];
   270→        if (s->forward_action == NULL)
   271→            return SAGA_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
   272→        /* Compensation function may be NULL (irreversible step). */
   273→    }
   274→    return SAGA_OK;
   275→}
   276→
   277→/* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   278→ *  Experimental Feature Flags
   279→ * ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   280→#ifdef EDUPAY_SAGA_ENABLE_TRACE_IDS
   281→#include <uuid/uuid.h>
   282→
   283→/**
   284→ * saga_trace_id_t:
   285→ *    128-bit identifier used for distributed tracing in labs that integrate
   286→ *    OpenTelemetry.  Defined under a feature flag so core business rules
   287→ *    remain dependency-free unless professors opt-in.
   288→ */
   289→typedef struct
   290→{
   291→    uint8_t bytes[16];
   292→} saga_trace_id_t;
   293→#endif /* EDUPAY_SAGA_ENABLE_TRACE_IDS */
   294→
   295→#endif /* EDUPAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_CORE_SAGA_DEFINITION_H */

     4→ * @brief Application-layer query for retrieving the current ledger balance

     7→ *
     8→ * The public API in this header follows the “Clean Architecture” style:

    16→ * Typical usage from a delivery-mechanism (e.g., HTTP handler):
    17→ *
    18→ *      bursar_query_context_t *ctx = bursar_query_context_create(pg_pool);
    19→ *
    20→ *      get_account_balance_query_t  query  = {
    21→ *          .account_id    = "STU-2024-000123",
    22→ *          .preferred_ccy = "USD"
    23→ *      };
    24→ *
    25→ *      account_balance_dto_t  result;
    26→ *      epay_rc_t rc = get_account_balance_execute(&query, &result, ctx);
    27→ *
    28→ *      if (rc == EPAY_RC_OK) {
    29→ *          render_json(result);
    30→ *      } else {
    31→ *          translate_error(rc);
    32→ *      }
    33→ *
    34→ * The interface is thread-safe as long as the caller guarantees that
    35→ * a given context instance is not used concurrently by multiple threads.
    36→ */
    37→
    38→#ifndef EDUPAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_BURSAR_SERVICE_APPLICATION_QUERIES_GET_ACCOUNT_BALANCE_QUERY_H
    39→#define EDUPAY_LEDGER_ACADEMY_BURSAR_SERVICE_APPLICATION_QUERIES_GET_ACCOUNT_BALANCE_QUERY_H
    40→
    41→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

    47→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    48→/* Project-wide Error & Tracing Utilities                                    */

    55→
    56→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    57→/* DTOs (Data-Transfer-Objects)                                              */
    58→
    59→/**
    60→ * @struct account_balance_dto_t
    61→ *
    62→ * @brief Serializable representation of an account’s balance projection.
    63→ */
    64→typedef struct account_balance_dto_s
